source_name: European Banking Authority
title: The EBA publishes its final draft technical standards on material acquisitions, material transfers, mergers and divisions under the Capital Requirements Directive
clean_title: The EBA publishes its final draft technical standards on material acquisitions, material transfers, mergers and divisions under the Capital Requirements Directive
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published its final draft Regulatory Technical Standards (RTS) and Implementing Technical Standards (ITSs) on material acquisitions, transfers of assets or liabilities, mergers and divisions involving credit institutions or (mixed) financial holding companies under the Capital Requirements Directive (CRD). The standards aim to support banking consolidation and deepen EU market integration by clarifying supervisory expectations, reducing regulatory uncertainty, and promoting a consistent prudential assessment framework across the European Union.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: acquisitions capital directive divisions draft eba final its material mergers
normalized_title: the eba publishes its final draft technical standards on material acquisitions material transfers mergers and divisions under the capital requirements directive

source_name: European Banking Authority
title: ​The EBA consults on amendments to data collection for the 2027 market risk benchmarking exercise
clean_title: ​The EBA consults on amendments to data collection for the 2027 market risk benchmarking exercise
summary: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) today launched a consultation on amendments to the Implementing Technical Standards (ITS) governing the benchmarking of internal models and the standardised approach for market risk for the 2027 exercise. The proposed amendments aim to ensure that the benchmarking framework remains aligned with the evolving regulatory framework, while providing institutions and supervisors with adequate time to prepare for the implementation of the upcoming market risk requirements. This consultation runs until 3 September 2026.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: 2027 amendments benchmarking collection consults data eba exercise market risk
normalized_title: the eba consults on amendments to data collection for the 2027 market risk benchmarking exercise
